topics.topic: Define Production, Productivity, Work study, Method Study and Work Measurement
State objectives of Work study, Method Study and Work Measurement
Explain the Steps: Define, Record, Critical Examination, Develop, and Install and maintain for conduct of method study to increase the productivity
Explain process chart symbols with examples
Explain the use of flow diagram and string diagram
Explain Cycle graph and Chrono cycle graph
Identify various therbligs used in micro motion study and construct SIMO chart
Explain Time study Procedure by using a stop watch
Compute the standard time for an operation by adding its constituent elements
Explain the procedure of Predetermined Motion Time Standards (PMTS)
Explain the method of conducting work sampling
State the terms involved in wage systems
List different incentive plans and solve simple numerical problems

WORK STUDY Industrial Engineering: Definition of Production and productivity; Work Study: Definition, objectives of work study. Method Study: Definition, objectives, procedure of conducting method study. Process chart symbols – Explanation with Operation process chart, Flow process chart and Two-handed process charts only - Uses of flow diagram and string diagram. Micro-motion study: Therbligs – Simo chart – Cycle graph and Chronocycle graph Work Measurement or Time study: Definition, objectives - Work measurement techniques. Time Study: Procedure by using a stop watch to measure the standard time - Constituents of standard time: Observed time - Normal time- rating factor- allowances. Standard data method - Determination of standard time by using Predetermined Motion Time Standards (PMTS) - Determination of standard time by using work sampling. Wage and Incentive plans: Definitions of wage, nominal wage, real wage, living wage, minimum wage, fair wage - Incentives and types - List different incentive plans - Halsey, Rowan and Emerson efficiency plans, solve simple numerical problems
